input.issue_title: Jitter when subscribing/unsubscribing to a channel
input.issue_body:
<!-- Issue description -->

As [reported on CZO](https://chat.zulip.org/#narrow/channel/9-issues/topic/.F0.9F.8E.AF.20jitter.20when.20subscribing.2Funsubscribing.20to.20a.20channel/near/1814234), when you subscribe/unsubscribe to a channel using the "Subscribe"/"Unsubscribe" button in the channel management UI, there's some visual jitter on the plus/checkmark in the channel list on the left. We should fix this.

WIP PR for this issue: #30845

<!-- Link to a message in the chat.zulip.org discussion. Message links will still work even if the topic is renamed or resolved. Link back to this issue from the chat.zulip.org thread. -->

[CZO thread](https://chat.zulip.org/#narrow/channel/9-issues/topic/.F0.9F.8E.AF.20jitter.20when.20subscribing.2Funsubscribing.20to.20a.20channel/near/1814234)

Unpredictable spaces render in the UI owing to a legacy space character in the `inline_decorated_channel_name.hbs` partial between `channel-privacy-type-icon` and `decorated-channel-name`. While it appears we're using CSS to add space to the right of the icon in that modal header, other places--such as the dropdown in the same modal--rely on the space: The likely workable solution: 1. Remove the whitespace character 2. Come up with em-based `padding` values for all `.channel-privacy-type-icon` instances 3. Verify (extensively) that there aren't any regressions in the numerous places the `inline_decorated_channel_name.hbs` partial gets rendered CZO thread: [#issues > channel privacy icon spacing in modal headers](https://chat.zulip.org/#narrow/channel/9-issues/topic/channel.20privacy.20icon.20spacing.20in.20modal.20headers/with/2322349)

input.issue_title: Clear channel name from right-side channel settings panel
input.issue_body:
In certain situations, when no stream is selected in stream settings, we continue to show the name of the previously selected stream. For example, this happens when a user unsubscribes from all the streams in the organization (after reloading the page):

![Screen Shot 2022-07-07 at 11 51 20 AM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2090066/177853223-07b3911b-58cc-47c3-95be-09aeb68e37cb.png)

We should clear the stream name from the right-side panel when the stream is unselected. When no stream is selected, we should show the "Stream settings" header in the right-side panel, as we do in other situations.

![Screen Shot 2022-07-07 at 11 52 51 AM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2090066/177853278-1e665737-a88c-438c-855a-28d1672765d7.png)

input.pr_title: push_registration: Clean up legacy token on E2EE registration.
input.pr_body:
CZO: [#api design > E2EE - clear legacy token registration](https://chat.zulip.org/#narrow/channel/378-api-design/topic/E2EE.20-.20clear.20legacy.20token.20registration/with/2407756) When a client successfully registers for E2EE push notifications, automatically remove any matching legacy `PushDeviceToken` and `RemotePushDeviceToken` registration with the same token for the user.
Self-review checklist - [x] [Self-reviewed](https://zulip.readthedocs.io/en/latest/contributing/code-reviewing.html#how-to-review-code) the changes for clarity and maintainability (variable names, code reuse, readability, etc.). - [x] Followed the [AI use policy](https://zulip.readthedocs.io/en/latest/contributing/contributing.html#ai-use-policy-and-guidelines). Communicate decisions, questions, and potential concerns. - [x] Explains differences from previous plans (e.g., issue description). - [x] Highlights technical choices and bugs encountered. - [x] Calls out remaining decisions and concerns. - [x] Automated tests verify logic where appropriate. Individual commits are ready for review (see [commit discipline](https://zulip.readthedocs.io/en/latest/contributing/commit-discipline.html)). - [x] Each commit is a coherent idea. - [x] Commit message(s) explain reasoning and motivation for changes. Completed manual review and testing of the following: - [ ] Visual appearance of the changes. - [ ] Responsiveness and

diff --git a/api_docs/changelog.md b/api_docs/changelog.md
index 505c8ac16c3c2..1499eb90b2482 100644
--- a/api_docs/changelog.md
+++ b/api_docs/changelog.md
@@ -20,6 +20,13 @@ format used by the Zulip server that they are interacting with.
 
 ## Changes in Zulip 12.0
 
+**Feature level 483**
+

+  successful E2EE push device registration, the server now
+  automatically removes any legacy push device registration with
+  a matching token for the user.
+
 **Feature level 482**
 
 * [`GET /messages`](/api/get-messages), [`GET
diff --git a/version.py b/version.py
index 4248f4759c172..f2fd13898eeb6 100644
--- a/version.py
+++ b/version.py
@@ -33,7 +33,7 @@
 # https://zulip.readthedocs.io/en/latest/documentation/api.html#step-by-step-guide
 # Also available at docs/documentation/api.md.
 
-API_FEATURE_LEVEL = 482
+API_FEATURE_LEVEL = 483
 
 # Bump the minor PROVISION_VERSION to indicate that folks should provision
 # only when going from an old version of the code to a newer version. Bump
diff --git a/zerver/lib/push_registration.py b/zerver/lib/push_registration.py
index bab840af6366b..9576390dae6bf 100644
--- a/zerver/lib/push_registration.py
+++ b/zerver/lib/push_registration.py
@@ -1,5 +1,6 @@
 import base64
 import binascii
+import hashlib
 import logging
 from typing import TypedDict
 
@@ -14,6 +15,7 @@
     MissingRemoteRealmError,
     RequestExpiredError,
 )
+from zerver.lib.push_notifications import remove_push_device_token
 from zerver.lib.remote_server import (
     PushNotificationBouncerError,
     PushNotificationBouncerRetryLaterError,
@@ -21,6 +23,7 @@
     send_to_push_bouncer,
 )
 from zerver.models.devices import Device
+from zerver.models.push_notifications import PushDeviceToken
 from zerver.models.users import UserProfile, get_user_profile_by_id
 from zerver.tornado.django_api import send_event_on_commit
 
@@ -67,6 +70,49 @@ def handle_registration_to_bouncer_failure(
         logging.error("Push registration request for device_id=%s expired.", device_id)
 
 
+def cleanup_legacy_push_device_token(user_profile: UserProfile, token_id_int: int) -> None:
+    MAX_REQUEST_RETRIES = 3
+    legacy_tokens = PushDeviceToken.objects.filter(user=user_profile)
+
+    for legacy_token in legacy_tokens:
+        hash_bytes = hashlib.sha256(legacy_token.token.encode()).digest()
+        computed_token_id_base64 = base64.b64encode(hash_bytes[0:8]).decode()
+        computed_token_id_int = b64decode_token_id_base64(computed_token_id_base64)
+
+        if computed_token_id_int != token_id_int:
+            continue
+
+        for attempt in range(MAX_REQUEST_RETRIES):
+            try:
+                remove_push_device_token(user_profile, legacy_token.token, legacy_token.kind)
+                return
+            except (
+                PushNotificationBouncerRetryLaterError,
+                PushNotificationBouncerServerError,
+            ) as e:
+                # Network error or 5xx error response from bouncer server.
+                # TODO: Retry with backoff. Implement it while working on #35853.
+                if attempt == MAX_REQUEST_RETRIES - 1:
+                    logger.warning(
+                        "Failed to remove legacy push token from bouncer "
+                        "for user %s after retries: %s",
+                        user_profile.id,
+                        e,
+                    )
+                    return
+            except PushNotificationBouncerError as e:
+                # Invalid credentials or unexpected status code
+                logger.error(
+                    "Bouncer error cleaning up legacy push token for user %s: %s",
+                    user_profile.id,
+                    e,
+                )
+                return
+            except JsonableError:
+                # Token doesn't exist i.e. already cleaned up.
+                return
+
+
 def handle_register_push_device_to_bouncer(
     queue_item: RegisterPushDeviceToBouncerQueueItem,
 ) -> None:
@@ -124,8 +170,9 @@ def handle_register_push_device_to_bouncer(
         return
 
     # Registration successful.
+    token_id_int = b64decode_token_id_base64(token_id_base64)
     Device.objects.filter(id=device_id).update(
-        push_token_id=b64decode_token_id_base64(token_id_base64), pending_push_token_id=None
+        push_token_id=token_id_int, pending_push_token_id=None
     )
     event = dict(
         type="device",
@@ -136,6 +183,10 @@ def handle_register_push_device_to_bouncer(
     )
     send_event_on_commit(user_profile.realm, event, [user_profile.id])
 
+    # When a client successfully registers a token for E2EE notifications,
+    # clear out any existing legacy registration on that account for the same token.
+    cleanup_legacy_push_device_token(user_profile, token_id_int)
+
 
 def check_push_key(push_key_str: str) -> bytes:
     error_message = _("Invalid `push_key`")
diff --git a/zerver/openapi/zulip.yaml b/zerver/openapi/zulip.yaml
index 343deed0856f0..1c9d3d999ec31 100644
--- a/zerver/openapi/zulip.yaml
+++ b/zerver/openapi/zulip.yaml
@@ -13415,9 +13415,22 @@ paths:
         - **Rotate push key**: Provide only the push key fields.
         - **Rotate token**: Provide only the token fields.
 
-        **Changes**: In Zulip 12.0 (feature level 468), the endpoint
-        was significantly redesigned to support rotation of `push_key` and
-        token provided by FCM/APNs.
+        On a successful registration, the server automatically removes
+        any legacy push device registration with a matching token for
+        the user. For self-hosted servers, if removing the legacy
+        registration from the push notification bouncer fails (e.g.,
+        due to a network error), legacy notifications to that token
+        will continue until all of the user's legacy registrations
+        have been removed from the local server, at which point the
+        server will stop sending l

input.pr_title: [ai] user_card_popover: Pass user_id in toggle popover for message.
input.pr_body:
Prep for defer loading user data. Reduce the number of `get_by_user_id` calls so that there
is less number of call sites we need to worry about.

This can be independently merged from #38821

---

Asked claude to identify changes we can make independent of #38821 for the complete plan.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

input.pr_title: settings: Update channel group and user group to use view-only styling.
input.pr_body: ### Changes: **Channel settings menu** - Updated the channel description field to use the view-only CSS. - Added "Edit channel name and description" button to the right of the channel title in the right pane header. - Simplified channel property alert notifications as per discussed [here](https://chat.zulip.org/#narrow/channel/101-design/topic/uneditable.20field.20styling/near/2412880)[#design > uneditable field styling @ 💬](https://chat.zulip.org/#narrow/channel/101-design/topic/uneditable.20field.20styling/near/2412880). - Moved the channel property alert notifications to the channel setting title. If the screen isn't wide enough, it'll jump to the right pane body. **User group settings menu** - Updated the user group description field to use the view-only CSS. - If the group doesn't have a description, it falls back to "No description" string, just like the fallback render for empty Channel description. Design discussion: [#design > uneditable field styling](https://chat.zulip.org/#narrow/channel/101-design/topic/uneditable.20field.20styling/with/2310316) **Screenshots and screen captures:** - 1 line description (Updated on March 27th): | Before| After | |--------|--------| | | | | | | - Long description (Updated on March 30th): | Before | After | |--------|--------| | |

This reverts commit 220d3320e1329e79ae8444758940f826efe6b8e0 (#38693).

The issue was fixed upstream.
